scenes.text: The threshold for being a low income country was 610 USD in 1990. Countries with GDP per capita below this level were classified as low income. 49 countries belonged to the group of low-income countries. That corresponded to 3 out of every 10 countries.
The threshold for being a low income country is updated annually for inflation. By 2024 the threshold stood at 1,135 USD.
Compared to 1990, many countries managed to move to a higher income class by 2024.
28 countries managed to escape low income - Guyana even making it to high income.
However, five additional countries have since been classified as low income.

The countries currently classified as low income have had average GDP per capita growth rates of 0.47 percent per year since 1981. A recent Global Economic Prospects report by the World Bank (January 2025) finds that their outlook is bleak. Based on 2010-2019 growth rates only six of the current 25 LICs are on a path of becoming middle-income even by 2050.
